schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Observables & Diagnostics to Extract the Pull of Each Arm: Γ_H(t)

theory.canonical_statement: # 7) Observables & diagnostics to extract the "pull" of each arm - **Circulation of hemisphere \(H\)**: \(\Gamma_H(t)=\iint_{H} \zeta(\theta,\lambda,t)\, R^2\sin\theta\,d\theta\,d\lambda\). Compare \(\Gamma_{N}\) vs \(\Gamma_{S}\). - **Streamline topology**: count closed streamlines encircling each pole; compute winding number of specific streamlines. - **Energy/Enstrophy spectra**: look at spectral energy \(E_l=\tfrac{1}{2}\sum_m \frac{|\Psi_{l,m}|^2 l(l+1)}{R^2}\) to see where energy sits (dipole is l=1; waves are higher l). - **PV anomalies**: measure \(\Delta q\) from mean to see wave-induced PV flux across the equator.
theory.technical_interpretation: Observables & Diagnostics to Extract the Pull of Each Arm: Γ_H(t) is an algebraic definition, written Γ_H(t)=iint_H ζ(θ,λ,t) R^2sinθ dθ dλ. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from ζ, θ, λ, H, R, defining Γ_H(t). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
